close_reason: Already fixed: README lines 8, 11, 29 say project-name, not project-id/UUID.
description: secretx-bitwarden/README.md:8 documents the URI as 'secretx:bitwarden:<project-id>/<secret-name>' and describes project-id as a 'Bitwarden project UUID'. The example at README.md:29 uses a UUID literal. But the implementation in src/lib.rs:100-105 splits the path into <project-name>/<secret-name>, then resolve_project_id (lib.rs:238-256) lists all projects in the org and finds one whose .name field matches the supplied string. The rustdoc in src/lib.rs:11 and the example at src/lib.rs:22 correctly say 'project-name'. The README will mislead users into thinking a UUID is required, and a user who follows the README and pastes a UUID will get NotFound (because no project's name equals the UUID string). Either: (a) update the README to match the code (preferred — the lookup-by-name UX is more friendly), or (b) change the code to accept either a UUID or a name. Option (a) is the smaller change.
title: README claims project-id (UUID), code uses project-name (string)
